---
title: "Live: Bundibugyo Ebolavirus Evidence Surveillance"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

This page displays the current evidence surveillance for Bundibugyo Ebolavirus, covering publications from January 1, 2026 to present.

Evidence surveillance is a systematic approach to identifying and characterizing literature on a topic through a continuous cycle, following the principles of evidence synthesis research. This evidence surveillance collects research on Ebola virus, with a focus on Bundibugyo ebolavirus and the 2026 outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and surrounding areas.

For further information on our approach to evidence surveillance please see [Corrin et al. (2023)](https://doi.org/10.14745/ccdr.v49i01a02)  

**Contact email**: evidence.synthesis-synthese.de.donnees.probantes@phac-aspc.gc.ca.  

## Search strategy

The search was developed by the Health Canada library to capture Bundibugyo ebolavirus literature. It was implemented in Embase, Medline, Global Health, Scopus, and EuropePMC to capture preprints. The search is run twice per week to capture new publications.

Example search: 
```text
(TITLE_ABS:(((Bundibugyo or BDBV) AND (ebola virus* or virus* or viral* or ebola* or EBOV or EVD or EBOD)) or BEBOV) OR BODY:(((Bundibugyo or BDBV) AND (ebola virus* or virus* or viral* or ebola* or EBOV or EVD or EBOD)) or BEBOV) OR KEYWORD:(((Bundibugyo or BDBV) AND (ebola virus* or virus* or viral* or ebola* or EBOV or EVD or EBOD)) or BEBOV)) AND (FIRST_PDATE:2026)
```

A grey literature search has also been developed to identify new guidelines, evidence syntheses, risk assessments and other publications mainly from governments and international organizations:
CDC, ECDC, WHO, UKHSA, PHAC, PAHO, and others as they are identified by a daily Google search "Bundibugyo virus" OR "Bundibugyo Ebola" OR "BDBV" to identify additional publications omitted by the search.

## Screening

- All steps of screening are conducted by one reviewer and randomly checked by a senior reviewer
- All citations from the search are screened for relevance to ebolavirus 
- All ebolavirus full texts are screened for ebola virus strain covered in the paper. Bundibugyo ebolavirus and the current outbreak research are screened through for [further characterization.](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1L2zUU8NAKgkD-jkk1Carkbol3w6TlcNo/view?usp=sharing)

Characteristics of the publication are captured: language of the article, primary research or type of non-primary research, and publication status (published, pre-print, report)

## Evidence Map
- Identifies papers related to the 2026 outbreak associated with the bundibugyo ebolavirus
- Identifies the samples/subjects in the study: humans, animals, in vitro or in silico AND within humans identifies high risk populations (children <18, elderly >65, pregnant persons, immunocompromised, indigenous, ethnic minorities, healthcare workers and other options).
- The mode of transmission is captured if reported / relevant to the paper
- The high level foci of the paper is captured as: Epidemiological parameters,  Clinical Data,  Treatment /Prophylaxis,  Immunity,  Other vaccine studies,  Diagnostic / Detection test performance,  Interventions (PHM and IPC),  Genomics and structural characterization,  Knowledge, attitudes and behaviour,  Other epidemiology (e.g. emergence and spread), Indirect impacts of the disease 

**The table below is generated from the CSV file stored in this outbreak folder:**

`outbreaks/bundibugyo-ebola/data/references.csv`

**Note:** the literature list includes all ebola research identified, however only bundibugyo literature is fully characterized.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

rawReferences = FileAttachment("data/references.csv").csv({typed: false})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

visibleColumns = rawReferences.length > 0
  ? Object.keys(rawReferences[0]).filter(c =>
      c &&
      c.trim() !== "" &&
      !/^_+$/.test(c.trim()) &&
      !c.startsWith("__") &&
      !c.toLowerCase().startsWith("unnamed")
    )
  : []
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

references = rawReferences.map(row =>
  Object.fromEntries(
    visibleColumns.map(column => [column, row[column]])
  )
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

html`<div class="status-box">
<strong>${references.length}</strong> records loaded from the current CSV file.<br>
<strong>${visibleColumns.length}</strong> columns detected in this dataset.
</div>`
```

## Search the evidence

Use the search box below to search across all fields in the dataset.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof searched_references = Inputs.search(
  references,
  {placeholder: "Search all fields..."}
)

```
<details class="filter-panel">
<summary><strong>Filter the evidence</strong></summary>

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

cleanValue = value =>
  value === null || value === undefined ? "" : String(value).trim()
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

filterableColumns = visibleColumns.filter(column =>
  column &&
  column.trim() !== "" &&
  !/^_+$/.test(column.trim()) &&
  column.trim() !== "__evidence"
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

filterOptions = Object.fromEntries(
  filterableColumns.map(column => [
    column,
    ["All", ...Array.from(
      new Set(
        references
          .map(row => cleanValue(row[column]))
          .filter(value => value !== "")
      )
    ).sort()]
  ])
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

viewof selectedFilters = Inputs.form(
  Object.fromEntries(
    filterableColumns.map(column => [
      column,
      Inputs.select(filterOptions[column], {label: column})
    ])
  )
)
```

</details>

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

filtered_references = searched_references.filter(row =>
  filterableColumns.every(column =>
    selectedFilters[column] === "All" ||
    cleanValue(row[column]) === selectedFilters[column]
  )
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

html`<div class="status-box">
<strong>${filtered_references.length}</strong> records shown after search and filters.
</div>`
```
## Browse table

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

Inputs.table(filtered_references, {
  columns: visibleColumns,
  height: 700,
  select: false,
  format: {
    URL: value =>
      value
        ? htl.html`<a href=${value} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">View link</a>`
        : "",

    DOI: value =>
      value
        ? htl.html`<a href=${`https://doi.org/${value}`} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">${value}</a>`
        : ""
  }
})
```

<div class="download-buttons">
<a href="data/references.csv" download="bundibugyo-ebola-key-columns.csv" class="btn btn-primary">
Download key columns CSV
</a>

<a href="data/full-dataset.xlsx" download="bundibugyo-ebola-full-dataset.xlsx" class="btn btn-primary">
Download full Excel dataset
</a>
</div>

::: {.status-box}
**Note:** This outbreak page uses a generic CSV viewer. Columns may differ across outbreaks depending on the evidence surveillance output.
:::
